other_uses: Iconic blue-flowered camas whose wet meadows were carefully tended food fields across the Northwest.
prep: Pit-cook the bulbs for a day or more to turn them sweet and digestible — one of the most important Indigenous root crops of the West. CRITICAL: deadly 'death camas' grows in the same meadows; dig only when the blue flowers confirm the ID.
soil: Moist to wet meadows; seasonally wet soils.
risks: Deadly 'death camas' look-alike — dig only when flowering (blue).
buy: Native-plant nurseries and native seed suppliers.
